criteria.name: Identification of Reaction Components
criteria.description: Ability to correctly identify and articulate the reactants, products, and intermediates involved in the reaction.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all relevant reactants, products, and intermediates with appropriate chemical names and structures.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify key reactants or products, or mislabels intermediates.

criteria.name: Clarification of Reaction Conditions
criteria.description: Ability to specify the conditions under which the reaction occurs, such as temperature, solvent, and catalysts.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Clearly specifies all relevant reaction conditions, demonstrating an understanding of their impact on the reaction.
criteria.poor_indicator: Omits important reaction conditions or misunderstands their role in the mechanism.

criteria.name: Definition of Reaction Pathway
criteria.description: Ability to outline a step-by-step pathway for the reaction, identifying key mechanistic steps.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides a detailed and correct step-by-step pathway, identifying each mechanistic step accurately.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides an incomplete or incorrect pathway, missing key steps or misidentifying mechanistic processes.

typical_errors: Misidentifying the starting materials or products.
Neglecting to consider the role of catalysts or solvents.

criteria.name: Error Identification in Reaction Mechanism
criteria.description: Ability to detect errors or inaccuracies in the proposed reaction mechanism.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all errors or inaccuracies in the mechanism, including incorrect electron flow or missing intermediates.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to detect major errors in the mechanism or incorrectly identifies correct steps as errors.

criteria.name: Assessment of Reaction Feasibility
criteria.description: Ability to judge whether the proposed reaction mechanism is chemically feasible.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly assesses the feasibility of the mechanism based on chemical principles and known reaction behavior.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misjudges the feasibility, either accepting implausible steps or rejecting plausible ones.

criteria.name: Evaluation of Stereochemistry
criteria.description: Ability to evaluate and identify any stereochemical issues in the mechanism.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately evaluates stereochemistry, identifying any errors or omissions in stereochemical assignments.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks stereochemical considerations or misidentifies stereochemical outcomes.

typical_errors: Failing to recognize incorrect electron flow.

criteria.name: Correction of Mechanistic Errors
criteria.description: Ability to provide accurate corrections for identified errors in the mechanism.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides precise and chemically sound corrections for all identified errors in the mechanism.
criteria.poor_indicator: Offers vague or incorrect corrections, failing to address the root cause of the errors.

criteria.name: Optimization of Reaction Pathway
criteria.description: Ability to suggest improvements to the reaction pathway for greater efficiency or yield.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Suggests logical and effective improvements that enhance the efficiency or yield of the reaction.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides ineffective or unrealistic suggestions that do not improve the mechanism.

criteria.name: Clarification and Justification of Changes
criteria.description: Ability to clearly explain and justify the changes made to the mechanism.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Thoroughly explains and justifies all changes with reference to chemical principles and evidence.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to provide clear explanations or justifications for changes, or relies on incorrect reasoning.

typical_errors: Proposing changes that violate fundamental chemical principles.
Failing to consider the impact of changes on reaction yield.
